subject: Cloud Computing
title: The Infinite Storage Illusion
description: One-minute skill: The Infinite Storage Illusion.

hook.text: Cloud storage feels infinite. Upload anything, anytime, no limit. But behind the illusion, physical disks are spinning.

buildup.text: Cloud providers manage exabytes across millions of drives. When drives fail, data is automatically re-replicated.

discovery.text: The illusion works because of scale. Individual failures are invisible when billions of operations succeed daily.

twist.text: But the physical world has limits. Energy, rare earth minerals, and water for cooling are all finite resources.

climax.text: Cloud storage growth is exponential. Whether the planet can sustain it forever is the question nobody wants to answer.

punchline.text: Storage feels infinite. The planet providing it is not.

quiz.question: Why does cloud storage appear infinite to users?
options: Massive scale makes individual hardware failures invisible to customers
Cloud providers have discovered how to create storage from nothing
There are no physical limits to how much data can be stored
